steps.title: Map the text's three-part structure
steps.content: The passage moves through three beats: (1) an obstacle — the NCP is discernible only at night; (2) an inspiration — researchers were inspired by the navigational strategies of insects and birds that use skylight polarization; (3) a solution — researchers mimicked that ability with a polarimetric camera that captures polarization patterns to locate the NCP in daytime. Every correct description must capture all three beats in order.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice against the text
steps.content:
Check which description accurately matches the obstacle $\Rightarrow$ inspiration $\Rightarrow$ solution structure:
  • Choice A — 'It illustrates how most navigational tools utilize the NCP...': the text never says most navigational tools use the NCP, and it never says researchers discovered animals navigate without it — animals use polarization to find the NCP. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It presents a celestial-based method of navigation, enumerates comparative benefits...': the text does not compare the accuracy of animal vs. human methods, and the animal strategy is not "unrelated" to the NCP. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It explains how the NCP is typically located, emphasizes a key difference between how humans and animals use the NCP...': the text says nothing about how humans typically locate the NCP, and it does not propose augmenting existing navigational instruments. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It notes an obstacle to observing an astronomical phenomenon, mentions a navigational ability of certain animals that inspired a solution...': matches all three beats exactly — obstacle (NCP only visible at night), inspiration (animal polarization navigation), and solution (polarimetric camera mimics that ability). ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
